rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. AAPSARA BHATT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Aapsara Bhatt, Jitendra R. Patel, Pritikumari J. Patel Aka Pritti J. Patel Individually Dba Bonfare Market 23'S Motion To Tax Costs And To Confirm Remaining Amount Of Debt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 26, 2009, line 18, Defendants Aapsara Bhatt, Jitendra R. Patel, Pritikumari J. Patel, aka, Pritti J. Patel, indivdually, dba, Bonfare Market 23's Motion to tax costs and to confirm remaining amount of debt. (transferred from Department 301, per Challenge to Judge Busch). Motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. Motion grantd as to $1,500 attorney fees from June 4, 2009 forward. Motion to tax those costs timely. Attorney fees unreasonable and unnecessary in view of small amount of the debt, the outcome of the motion, and the prior attorney fees of over $4,000. Motion is denied as to remainder. Motion untimely as to other issues and interest continues to accrue on the unpaid portion of the debt. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHANEL STASZ VS. ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 26, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT MICHAEL EISENBERG AS EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Motion to quash granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the genuine ignorance requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 474. Plaintiff's probate claim and its rejection indicate she knew the identity of the executor for the estate. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RISHI KAPILA MD VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Prohibition, Stay, And Other Appropriate Relief;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 26, 2009, line 12, PETITIONER RISHI KAPILA Notice Of Hearing on Petition for Writ of Prohibition, Stay, and Other Appropriate Relief. Petition for writ of prohibition is denied. Petitioner has not made any showing that respondents' reports are in excess of their jurisdiction. A report was sent to the American Board of Internal Medicine in July 2009 and the court cannot provide retrospective injunctive relief. The report to the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education focuses on programs, not on individual trainees. The names and status of trainees are maintained confidentially. Respondents release no other information to any other entities without doctors' consent. =(302/CWW)
